reasoning: Over a roughly 3-month horizon (November 2025 to February 2026), the 1-year expected inflation rate should follow a roughly random walk with a slight mean-reverting pull towards the Fed's 2% target. Because 1-year expectations cover a full 12-month period, seasonality effects typically cancel out. Given the starting point around 2.745%, which is slightly elevated relative to the long-term target, the series has a marginally higher likelihood of decreasing or staying steady than increasing. The probability of a strict increase over this short window is just under 50%.
